rows.description: Item to ye bringer of medenham and ladlane rent
rows.notes: Text and the three-column numbers at the far right are legible. The amount reads clearly as 0 pounds, 2 shillings, 0 pence; vertical ruling lines align this row with the other amounts.
rows.english_description: Payment to the messenger who brought in the rents from Medenham and Ladlane.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the messenger who brought in the rents from Medenham and Ladlane, amounting to £0 2s 0d.

rows.description: To mr Baron instead of a chamber
rows.notes: Amount reads as 1-0-0 in the £/s/d columns; writing is clear and aligned with the ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Baron in lieu of providing a chamber.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Baron in lieu of providing a chamber, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.description: To mr Beckner for cleaning Pictury and ye Kings arms
rows.notes: Description and amount (3£ 12s 0d) are crisply written and well aligned; shillings 12 and pence 0 clearly visible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Beckner for cleaning a picture and the King's arms.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Beckner for cleaning a picture and the King's arms, amounting to £3 12s 0d.

rows.description: To mr Collison for work and materials
rows.notes: Numbers (1£ 5s 8d) are clear; pence '8' shape is distinct. Vertical alignment with column guidelines confirmed.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Collison for work and materials.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Collison for work and materials, amounting to £1 5s 8d.

rows.notes: Amount appears as 2£ 6s 9d; digits are legible though the middle of the line has slight ink fade. Column alignment consistent with neighbors.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Lumly on two bills (settlement of two invoices).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Lumly on two bills, amounting to £2 6s 9d.

rows.notes: Pounds read as '10' and shillings '17', pence '9' — the digits are larger and slightly compressed; unusually large amount increases chance of mis-read but alignment supports this reading.
rows.english_description: Payment to Fifield the slater for roofing work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Fifield the slater for roofing work, amounting to £10 17s 9d.

rows.description: To mr Clark for paper, pens &c
rows.notes: Rightmost columns show 0£ 7s 11d clearly; the '11' pence is well-formed (two strokes).
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Clark for paper, pens and stationery.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Clark for paper, pens and stationery, amounting to £0 7s 11d.

rows.notes: Underlined subtotal reads '19 12 3' clearly; used as column alignment reference for the section above. Values validate (s <20, d <12).
Section heading (introduces several fine items). No amounts on the label row; handwriting moderately clear.

rows.description: For ye Brinkrouge in St Giles 13:4 mr Smiths of Kidlington 2.14.9 03
rows.notes: Description is crowded and contains multiple place/names; the rightmost amount is faint and partially smudged. Interpreted the three rightmost figures as 3£ 8s 1d — alternative readings are possible (ink blot near the shillings/pence). Chose this reading based on alignment with the Sum line above.
rows.english_description: Fine relating to the Brinkrouge in St Giles and a fine from Mr Smith of Kidlington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine relating to the Brinkrouge in St Giles and a fine from Mr Smith of Kidlington, amounting to £3 8s 1d.

rows.description: For mr walkers Tenent, 10? mr Hughes 12?
rows.notes: Amount appears as '01 2 0' and aligns with the shilling column. The descriptive text is abbreviated and partly obscured (handwriting compressed), but the numeric trio is reasonably clear.
rows.english_description: Fine concerning Mr Walker's tenant and a fine referenced for Mr Hughes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine concerning Mr Walker's tenant and a fine referenced for Mr Hughes, amounting to £1 2s 0d.

rows.notes: Bracketed group of names; this line shows 01 10 0 in the columns. Script for the name is slightly faded but numeric columns are aligned and clear.
rows.english_description: Annual payments of 15 shillings from Pears and 15 shillings at Michaelmas from Churchill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payments of 15 shillings from Pears and 15 shillings at Michaelmas from Churchill, recorded here as £1 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Second entry in the bracket with identical-looking amount '01 10 0'. The pence column is clearly 0.
rows.english_description: Annual payments from Jago at the Annunciation and at Michaelmas (each 15 shillings).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payments from Jago at the Annunciation and at Michaelmas, amounting here to £1 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount reads '00 15 0' (15 shillings). The '00' pounds column is faint but consistent with neighbouring zeros in the bracket.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Penrose of 15 shillings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Penrose of 15 shillings, amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Name 'Blake' is present under the bracket with no visible amount on its line — appears to be a sub-item of the bracketed fines (no separate amount written).
rows.english_description: Entry referencing Blake with no further details recorded.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Entry referencing Blake with no amount stated.

rows.notes: Section label (appears left of several rows of numeric entries). Handwriting of heading clear; no numeric amount on the header itself.

rows.description: Dno Rv? et socijs & Augment. red. Southern. et merton - Stipend. & Officiarys
rows.notes: First numeric line in the 'Ad Fest. Nativ.' block reads 22£ 0s 0d clearly. Some abbreviation in the description (Rv? / socijs) makes the label uncertain but the amount is distinct.
rows.english_description: Payments of stipends and officiary fees at Christmas to the rector/reverend and fellows, with reference to augmentations from Southern and Merton.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payments of stipends and officiary fees at Christmas to the rector/reverend and fellows, with reference to augmentations from Southern and Merton, amounting to £22 0s 0d.

rows.description: Ad Fest Ann. & Augm red. Cuddington 3s ptes - Stipend
rows.notes: Second line in the block reads '17 0 0' in the columns. The descriptive line is abbreviated and ink is lighter, so reading is moderately uncertain but numeric trio is aligned and plausible.
rows.english_description: Stipend at the Annunciation and portion of augmentation from Cuddington applied to stipend.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend at the Annunciation and portion of augmentation from Cuddington applied to stipend, amounting to £17 0s 0d.

rows.description: Ad Fest Bapt. 4th pte red Augm Cuddington
rows.notes: This row shows '36 14 2' in the £/s/d columns. The description is compact and contains abbreviations; numeric columns are readable but ink is a little uneven.
rows.english_description: One quarter share of the reduced augmentation from Cuddington at the feast of St John the Baptist.
rows.english_description_with_amount: One quarter share of the reduced augmentation from Cuddington at the feast of St John the Baptist, amounting to £36 14s 2d.

rows.notes: Follows previous descriptive line; amounts '28 6 8' are visible though the '6' shillings digit is slightly cramped. Column alignment supports this reading.
rows.english_description: Payment of stipends and officiary fees (general line).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of stipends and officiary fees, amounting to £28 6s 8d.

rows.notes: This line is a short descriptive continuation (illegible start) with amounts '12 4 4'. The text is faint; numeric trio is readable but the label is unclear, so confidence reduced.
rows.english_description: Continuation line for the payments relating to the Baptism/officiaries group.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Continuation of the Baptism/officiaries payments, amounting to £12 4s 4d.

rows.description: Ad Fest Mai & Augm. red. Longwitham
rows.notes: Amount appears as 16£ 6s 8d; the place-name 'Longwitham' is legible but the line is slightly faded. Column values validate and align with neighbors.
rows.english_description: Augmentation (reduced) from Longwitham recorded at the May feast.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation (reduced) from Longwitham recorded at the May feast, amounting to £16 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Pence column shows '5' with a small trailing loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny). Read as 33£ 0s 5½d. The fraction mark is faint but has the characteristic halfpenny loop.
rows.english_description: Unclear entry referencing abbreviated names 'Solw.' and 'Guinear' (possibly places or patrons); meaning uncertain.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Unclear entry referencing 'Solw.' and 'Guinear', amount recorded as £33 0s 5.5d.

rows.notes: There is a short isolated numeric line under the 'Ad Fest' block that reads '21 10 0' in the columns but the descriptor is entirely obscured by an ink blot/scribble. Treated as a total/subtotal; description unavailable.

rows.description: To mr Beckner for work att ye ArchBps of Armagh Picture
rows.notes: Description and amount '01 5 0' are readable; minor ink shadow from previous scribble but digits are clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Beckner for work on the Archbishop of Armagh's picture.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Beckner for work on the Archbishop of Armagh's picture, amounting to £1 5s 0d.

rows.description: To mr Taylor for a Frame for ye sd Picture
rows.notes: Clear line with amount '06 9 0'. Text and numbers are legible and aligned in the columns.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Taylor for a frame for the same picture.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Taylor for a frame for the same picture, amounting to £6 9s 0d.

rows.notes: Subtotal for the lower block reads approximately '203 1 4' but the pence digit area has a faint additional mark that could be a fraction or stray pen stroke. Chosen reading '4d' based on alignment; alternate readings possible.

Large total line shows '482 5 ?' — the final digit is ambiguous in the scan (looks like '19' but pence cannot be 19). Interpreted conservatively as 482£ 5s 1d; the pence digit is uncertain due to overlapping pen flourish.
Line reads '383 3 10' in the columns. There is a small stray mark to the right that looked like an extra digit but does not align with the pence column; accepted reading is 10d.

rows.description: Sic Introduxit hoc anno Dni: Computans.
rows.notes: The ledger insertion line reads clearly as '99 1 3'. The phrase labeling the entry is legible and standard formulaic text for carried totals.

rows.description: Computus suprascriptus examinatus & approbatum per nos
rows.notes: Certification header above signatures. No monetary amount on this row; handwriting is clear.
rows.section_header: Computus suprascriptus examinatus & approbatum per nos

rows.notes: Signature line clearly reads 'Sa: Adams'. Treated as a header/attestation row (no amount).
Second signature visible as 'Robt. Rous.'; legible and without numbers.
Signature-like line 'Gu: Paynter R.T.' visible; no amounts. Some flourish on initials reduces clarity of exact post-nominal letters but name is readable.
Signature 'Geo. Verman' followed by an abbreviated office (looks like 'Subrector'). Hand is more flourish-heavy here; read with moderate confidence.
Final signature line is heavily stylised and partly obscured by a fold/shadow; read approximately as 'Ric: Newbing' but letters uncertain. No amounts.
